zh.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: 英國素可列維斯列維斯 聖 安妮
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Vere於大約1255年出生在英國奧可福爾時，她的父親Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford36歲，母親Alice Sanford27歲。她於1285年6月10日與William de Warenne在英國英格蘭素瑞結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1293年11月21日在英國素可列維斯列維斯 聖 安妮享年39歲，埋葬在英國素可其斯特。

zh-hans.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: 英国素可列維斯列維斯 聖 安妮
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Vere于大约1255年出生在英国奧可福爾时，她的父亲Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford36岁，母亲Alice Sanford27岁。她于1285年6月10日与William de Warenne在英国英格兰素瑞结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1293年11月21日在英国素可列維斯列維斯 聖 安妮享年39岁，埋葬在英国素可其斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Vere was born about 1255, in Oxfordshire, England, her father, Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford, was 36 and her mother, Alice Sanford, was 27. She married William de Warenne on 10 June 1285, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 21 November 1293, in Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in Chichester, Sussex, England.

fr.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Vere est née environ 1255, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Alice Sanford, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé William de Warenne le 10 juin 1285, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 novembre 1293, à Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Chichester, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Vere um 1255 in Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford 36 und ihre Mutter Alice Sanford 27. Sie hat am 10. Juni 1285 in Surrey, England, United Kingdom William de Warenne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. November 1293 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England und wurde in Chichester, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Vere è nata il circa 1255 nel Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Alice Sanford, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato William de Warenne il 10 giugno 1285, nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 21 novembre 1293, nel Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Chichester, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Vereが約1255年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxfordは36歳，母Alice Sanfordは27歳であった。1285年6月10日にイギリスイギリススリーでWilliam de Warenneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1293年11月21日にイギリススセックスルスルス スト アンで39歳で死去し，イギリススセックスチチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: 영국 수세스 루스 루스 스트 앤
ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Vere(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Sanford(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1285년 6월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 William de Warenne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1293년 11월 21일 영국 수세스 루스 루스 스트 앤에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 치체스터에 묻혔다.

pt.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Vere nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Alice Sanford, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Warenne em 10 de junho de 1285, em Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de novembro de 1293, em Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Chichester, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, England: львесь ст анн, львесь, суссекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Vere родилась около 1255 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Vere 5th Earl of Oxford было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Sanford, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Warenne 10 июня 1285 г. в местности сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 ноября 1293 г. в местности львесь ст анн, львесь, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности шичестир, суссекс, Англия.

Murió el 21 de noviembre de 1293, en Lewes St Anne, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Chichester, Sussex, Inglaterra.
